name: Reflexion Agent
description: Act, self-grade the attempt, write the lesson to memory, and retry with that reflection in context — self-correction that remembers its failed attempts, unlike one-shot Reflection.

data.label: How it works
data.text: Reflexion ≠ Reflection. The Actor attempts the task, an llm-judge grades it, and on failure the lesson is written to Memory before retrying — so the next attempt reads WHY the last one failed. Memory turns blind retries into learning.

data.label: Self-Grade
data.scoringPrompt: Score the attempt. Pass if it fully and correctly solves the task; fail if it is incomplete or wrong, and say what was missing.

data.label: The reflexion loop
data.text: On 'fail' the lesson goes to Memory, which feeds the Actor on retry. On 'pass' it answers. The final allowed visit forces termination so retries are bounded.
